runs.llm_reason: From BB out of position on a connected, wet flop (Th5s6h) with very deep stacks, checking is the safest default without hero hole cards. The board favors the preflop raiser less than a dry board, but betting without knowing hand class risks building a pot on a texture that can continue well. A small lead can be reasonable with strong made hands or strong draws, but visible context alone does not justify an automatic stab.

Out of position on a coordinated low board with very deep stacks and no visible hand information, checking is the lowest-risk legal action. The board can interact well with the caller's range, so without knowing hero's cards there is no clear small-bet value/protection spot to prefer betting.

Out of position in a deep-SPR heads-up pot on a connected two-tone board, with no hand information to justify a bluff or value bet, checking is the lowest-risk and most flexible legal action. The board favors continuing ranges enough that a small lead is not mandatory from BB without a specific holding.

Out of position on a connected monotone-ish/wet low board (Th5s6h) with very deep SPR, the default low-risk line is to check rather than build a large pot without knowing hero's holding. Betting can be reasonable with some ranges, but without hand info and with only bet/all-in available, checking preserves range and avoids overcommitting. All-in is clearly inappropriate at this SPR.

No hero hole cards are visible, so there is no specific value or draw context to justify betting into a deep-SPR pot. Out of position on a connected low board (Th5s6h), checking is the safest legal default and keeps the pot controlled without assuming a strong range or hand advantage.
